title: Pastor Sam Ketcher: Repent With Us and Come to Abolition Day
author: Sam Ketcher
orientation: An Oklahoma Free Will Baptist pastor issues a sharp rebuke to American churches for tolerating abortion and calls them to gather at the Oklahoma State Capitol on February 9, 2021 (Abolition Day) to demand passage of SB495 and the immediate abolition of abortion. The piece is equal parts prophetic indictment and mobilization appeal aimed squarely at fellow clergy and congregations.
audiences: Pew-sitting Christians weighing whether to act
Pro-life advocates who stop short of abolition
Skeptics wondering why religious rhetoric drives the movement

questions.question: What event is Pastor Ketcher inviting churches to attend, and when is it?
questions.answer: He is calling churches to Abolition Day at the Oklahoma State Capitol in Oklahoma City on February 9th, where activists will demand legislators immediately end abortion in Oklahoma rather than continue to regulate it.
questions.quote: Would you also be willing to join us on February 9th at the State Capital in Oklahoma City to let your voices be heard in defense of the fatherless, in defense of the unborn?

questions.question: How does Ketcher characterize churches that ignore abortion?
questions.audience: Pew-sitting Christians weighing whether to act
questions.answer: He likens silent or accommodating churches to wartime traitors and oblivious nobles, accusing them of being absorbed by a culture of death rather than confronting it as the salt of the earth they are commanded to be.
questions.quote: Some churches have become like Hanoi Jane fawning over the instruments of death in the name of love and compassion.  Some churches are a modern day Tokyo Rose who seeks to demoralize any who dare cross the gate to say “babies are murdered here.” But some churches are just contemporary nobleman who abstain from the fight because their only concerns are land, deed, and titles.

questions.question: What biblical analogy does Ketcher use to force a moral choice on his readers?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocates who stop short of abolition
questions.answer: He invokes the judgment of Solomon, asking readers whether they would be the woman who pleads for the child's life or the one willing to let the child be cut in two—framing passive pro-lifers as collaborators with the executioners.
questions.quote: If King Solomon were to stand before you and say “bring me a sword and dividing the living child in two,” would you be the woman who says “Let the child live or the woman who says let the child die?

questions.question: What specific posture does Ketcher want churches to take during January?
questions.answer: He asks whether congregations will spend the full month of January in prayer for unborn children scheduled for execution in Oklahoma, treating the entire month as a sustained season of intercession leading up to Abolition Day.
questions.quote: would your church spend the whole month of January in prayer for the unborn awaiting their execution in Oklahoma?

questions.question: How does Ketcher describe the cultural disorientation surrounding abortion?
questions.audience: Skeptics wondering why religious rhetoric drives the movement
questions.answer: He paints contemporary America as a moral inversion where evil is treated as good, victims of abortion are dehumanized into 'a clump of cells,' and the public has been psychologically captured into defending its captors.
questions.quote: Like a bad case of Stockholm syndrome we convince ourselves that our captors are benevolent and that a tiny human is just a clump of cells.

questions.question: What is Ketcher's theological framing for why Christians must show up?
questions.audience: Pew-sitting Christians weighing whether to act
questions.answer: He grounds the call directly in Christ's command to love one's neighbor and care for 'the least of these,' arguing that whatever is done—or not done—to the unborn is done to Christ Himself.
questions.quote: Join us on Abolition Day like lives depend on it. Because in fact they do. Join us like Christ has commanded us. Because in fact He has. Whatsoever you have done to the least of these He says “you have done unto me”.

questions.question: What does Ketcher say is the demand abolitionists are bringing to elected officials?
questions.answer: They are demanding, without compromise, that elected officials immediately end the murder of the unborn in Oklahoma and pass abolition rather than incremental regulation.
questions.quote: Would you gather with us to demand action from those we elected to cease immediately the murder of the unborn in Oklahoma? To call, without compromise, for the abolition of Abortion.

questions.question: How does Ketcher critique churches that wave the Great Commission while doing nothing about abortion?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocates who stop short of abolition
questions.answer: He accuses them of substituting slogans for action, calling their version of Christianity 'the great omission' and saying they have been culturally hellenized into accepting child sacrifice.
questions.quote: Waving their banners siting the great commission, they live like the great omission. Professing to be good samaritans in reality they’ve become hellenized by a culture of death and child sacrifice.

related.title: BREAKING: SB495 Might be Heard in Senate HHS Committee on Feb. 3!
related.reason: Same week, same bill—provides the legislative context for why Ketcher is summoning churches to the Capitol on February 9.

related.title: OK Senate HHS Committee Votes in Favor of Murdering 10,000 Babies
related.reason: Reports the SB495 committee vote that occurred days before Abolition Day, showing the outcome Ketcher's mobilization was responding to.

related.title: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
related.reason: Companion February 2021 reflection on the SB495 fight that complements Ketcher's prophetic tone.

related.title: Letter to Oklahoma Senators From a Post-Abortive Mother
related.reason: Another direct appeal to Oklahoma legislators during the SB495 push, from a different but complementary perspective.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Documents the broader Oklahoma Baptist movement Ketcher, a Free Will Baptist pastor, is appealing to and helping to lead.

related.title: Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Leaders and Pastors Endorse Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Tracks Free Will Baptist clergy organizing for abolition, the same denominational network Ketcher belongs to.

related.title: King Josiah’s Smashmouth Abolitionism
related.reason: Develops the same biblical-prophetic indictment of churches tolerating child sacrifice that Ketcher leans on.
